svg.text: VIETNAMESE
HISTORY & INDOCHINA
VIETNAMESE ORIGINS
Austroasiatic family: related to Khmer, Mon, Muong
~111 BCE-939 CE: Chinese rule; massive Chinese loanword layer
Sino-Vietnamese vocabulary: ~60% of formal words from Chinese
939 CE: Independence after Battle of Bach Dang River
Chu nom: Vietnamese characters adapted from Chinese (~10th c.)
COLONIAL PERIOD
1651: Alexandre de Rhodes publishes Vietnamese-Latin dictionary
1885-1954: French Indochina; Quoc Ngu promoted by French
Ho Chi Minh: used Quoc Ngu to spread revolutionary literacy
~90M speakers; 13th most spoken language in world
ZHUANG HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ZHUANG IN GUANGXI
Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region: created 1958 for Zhuang people
Zhuang: indigenous Tai-Kadai people of Southern China
Proto-Tai: ancestors of Thai, Lao, and Zhuang split ~2000 years ago
Nong Zhigao: Zhuang leader who resisted Song dynasty (1052 CE)
VIETNAMESE-ZHUANG ANCIENT CONNECTIONS
Nanyue Kingdom (204-111 BCE): encompassed both Guangxi and Vietnam
Zhao Tuo (Trieu Da): ruled unified Vietnam + Guangxi region
Shared archaeological culture: Dong Son bronze drums found in both
Guangxi-Vietnam border: ~1000km; long shared history
ZHUANG TODAY
~17M speakers; China's largest ethnic minority
Most Zhuang are bilingual in Mandarin; Sawcuengh Latin rarely used
Zhuang language: taught in some schools; regional TV broadcasting
MAINLAND SE ASIA LANGUAGE FAMILY
Vietnamese (Austroasiatic) and Zhuang (Tai-Kadai): different families
But: same region, same ecology, same cultural complex
Both languages shaped by: Chinese culture, rice agriculture, river systems
Dong Son drums: shared Bronze Age heritage across both territories
PLATE 38d ALT · VIETNAMESE / ZHUANG · HISTORY
